authorEyebrow: Inspired by The Mind Illuminated · Culadasa

home.title: Meditation
home.subtitle: Train steady attention and clear awareness, one sit at a time.
home.noSessions: No sessions yet. Start whenever you're ready.

learn.title: Learn the framework
learn.subtitle: Three short pieces of context - read once, revisit as needed.
learn.attentionBody: Attention is selective and focused - it isolates one object. Peripheral awareness is global and contextual; it includes how aware you are of the state of your own mind. The practice trains both, in balance.
learn.gardenerBody: You can't pull a sapling to make it grow. Progress comes from clear intentions, not from force. Patience over willpower; intention over striving; every sit is the right sit.
learn.nonLinearBody: A single sit can move between stages. Stress, illness, or a hard day can drop you back. This is normal. You are not behind.

stages.title: The ten stages
stages.subtitle: Ten stages divided by four milestones.
stages.switchTo: Set this as my current stage
stages.milestone1Title: Milestone One - Continuous attention to the breath
stages.milestone3Title: Milestone Three - Effortless stability of attention
stages.milestone4Title: Milestone Four - Persistence of an adept's qualities

session.title: Start a sit
session.moodLabel: How do you feel right now? (1-10)
session.distractionLabel: What kind of distraction was present?
session.obstaclesLabel: Anything that pulled at you today

insights.title: Reflect
insights.subtitle: A quiet summary of your practice - no scores.
insights.mindWanderingDown: Catches have been about {{count}} per sit lower recently than in the prior two weeks.
insights.mindWanderingUp: Catches have been about {{count}} per sit higher recently than in the prior two weeks.
insights.mindWanderingFlat: Catches have stayed about the same across the last four weeks.

dailyLife.title: Daily life mindfulness
dailyLife.subtitle: Carry the qualities of the sit into the rest of the day. One line is enough.
dailyLife.archiveSubtitle: Private notes about how the practice shows up off the cushion.

breath-awareness.title: Breath awareness
breath-awareness.shortDescription: Anchor your attention to the breath, returning gently when the mind wanders.
instructions: Sit comfortably and close your eyes if it feels right.
Notice the breath where it feels strongest - nose, chest, or belly.
When you notice your mind has wandered, gently bring it back to the breath.
Continue at a relaxed pace until the timer ends.

body-scan.title: Body scan
body-scan.shortDescription: Move attention slowly from head to toe, noticing without trying to change.
instructions: Bring attention to the crown of your head.
Slowly move your awareness down through the face, neck, shoulders, arms, torso, legs, and feet.
Pause at each area for a few breaths, noticing sensation without judgement.

loving-kindness.title: Loving-kindness
loving-kindness.shortDescription: Direct warm wishes towards yourself and gradually outward.
instructions: Bring to mind someone you care about easily.
Silently wish them: may you be safe, may you be well, may you be at ease.
Direct the same wishes to yourself.
Extend the wishes to others - a neutral person, then someone difficult, then all beings.

observing-thoughts.title: Observing thoughts
observing-thoughts.shortDescription: Watch thoughts come and go without grabbing on to any.
instructions: Imagine thoughts as clouds drifting across the sky, or leaves floating down a stream.
Notice each thought, then let it pass without following it.
Return to noticing whenever you find yourself caught in a thought.

info.title: The mind can be trained.
info.subtitle: Ten stages. One breath at a time.
info.heroBody: This module follows The Mind Illuminated - a structured path through ten distinct stages, from establishing a daily habit to deep meditative mastery.
info.attentionBody: Selective and focused. Isolates one object - like the breath - for closer inspection.
info.awarenessBody: Global and contextual. Holds the background of your experience - including the state of your own mind.
info.patience: Patience, not force - you can't pull a sapling to make it grow.
info.intention: Intention, not willpower - clear intentions, repeated daily, do the work.
info.everySit: Every sit counts - even a restless sit is practice.
info.attribution: Based on The Mind Illuminated by Culadasa (John Yates, Ph.D.)

welcome.title: The path to awakening
welcome.subtitle: Ten stages, four milestones - a practice, not a finish line.
welcome.body: The Mind Illuminated organizes meditative training as a sequence of ten distinct stages. The path is not linear: a single sit can move between stages, and a hard day can drop you back. That's normal.

attention.title: Attention and peripheral awareness
attention.subtitle: Two modes of knowing - the practice trains both.
attention.attentionBody: Selective and focused. Isolates one object - like the breath - for closer inspection.
attention.awarenessBody: Global and contextual. Includes outward sensory awareness and a quiet introspective sense of what your mind is doing.

assessment.title: Where are you starting?
assessment.subtitle: Five short questions. When in doubt, we'll land you a stage earlier rather than later.
assessment.habitQuestion: Do you have a daily sit habit already?
assessment.lengthQuestion: How long can you usually stay with the breath before noticing you've drifted?
assessment.sleepQuestion: Do you tend to fall asleep when you meditate?
assessment.catchQuestion: Can you notice you've been distracted before you've fully forgotten the breath?
assessment.noThoughtsQuestion: Have you experienced extended periods of breath sensation with no thoughts at all?

gardener.title: The gardener's mindset
gardener.subtitle: Three short reminders for the path.
gardener.patience: Patience over force - you can't pull a sapling to make it grow.
gardener.intention: Intention over willpower - clear intentions, repeated, do the work.
gardener.everySit: Every sit is the right sit - even the ones that feel bad are practice.

commit.title: Commit to a slot
commit.subtitle: Pick a daily time and a length. You can change both at any time.
commit.stageHint: Based on your answers, Stage {{stage}} is suggested - you can override it.
commit.reminderHint: Off by default. You can always change this in settings.
commit.error: Could not save. Please try again.

s1.title: Stage 1 - Establishing a Practice
s1.goal: Develop a regular, consistent meditation practice.
s1.obstacles: Resistance, procrastination, fatigue, impatience, boredom, lack of motivation.
s1.skills: Building routines and setting specific practice goals. The Six-Point Preparation primes each sit; the Four-Step Transition gently narrows attention into the breath.
s1.mastery: Never missing a daily practice session except when truly unavoidable.

s2.title: Stage 2 - Overcoming Mind-Wandering
s2.goal: Shorten mind-wandering and extend periods of sustained attention to the breath.
s2.skills: Reinforcing the 'aha' moment of catching mind-wandering - appreciating it, not judging it. Gently redirecting attention to the breath.
s2.mastery: Attention can stay on the breath for minutes while mind-wandering lasts only seconds.

prompts.wanderingCount: About how many times did you catch yourself off the breath?
prompts.shortening: Were the off-breath periods getting shorter through the sit?
prompts.pulledHard: Anything that pulled you off particularly hard today?

s3.title: Stage 3 - Overcoming Forgetting
s3.goal: Overcome forgetting the breath and falling asleep.
s3.skills: Following the breath (full inhale and exhale arc) and connecting (comparing breaths). Labeling distractions and periodically checking in on attention.
s3.mastery: Rarely forgetting the breath or falling asleep.

prompts.lostBreath: Did you lose the breath entirely at any point?
prompts.checkIn: Did you check in on your attention during the sit?

s4.title: Stage 4 - Overcoming Gross Distraction and Strong Dullness
s4.short: Overcoming gross distraction and strong dullness
s4.goal: Overcome gross distraction and strong dullness.
s4.obstacles: Distractions, pain and discomfort, intellectual insights, emotionally charged visions and memories.
s4.skills: Continuous introspective awareness - catching subtle problems before they grow. Learning to take pain as a meditation object. Old emotional material may surface; let it be.
s4.mastery: Gross distractions no longer push the breath into the background; breath sensations don't fade due to strong dullness.

prompts.grossDistraction: Was there a distraction that took over the breath today?
prompts.faintBreath: Did the breath ever feel faint, vague, or distorted?
prompts.charged: Did anything emotionally heavy surface during the sit?

s5.title: Stage 5 - Overcoming Subtle Dullness
s5.goal: Overcome subtle dullness and increase the power of mindfulness.
s5.obstacles: Subtle dullness is hard to recognize, creates an illusion of stability, and is seductively pleasant.
s5.skills: Vigilant introspective awareness to detect subtle dullness. The body-scanning technique refreshes mindfulness without sacrificing stability.
s5.mastery: Mindfulness can be sustained or even increased through the course of each sit.

prompts.vivid: Were breath sensations as vivid at the end of the sit as at the start?
prompts.peripheral: Did peripheral awareness stay alive during the sit?
prompts.bodyScan: Did you run a body scan today?

s6.title: Stage 6 - Subduing Subtle Distractions
s6.goal: Subdue subtle distractions and develop metacognitive introspective awareness.
s6.obstacles: Subtle thoughts and mental objects alternate with the breath in peripheral awareness.
s6.skills: Defining the scope of attention precisely. The 'whole body with the breath' technique keeps the breath central while keeping peripheral awareness alive.
s6.mastery: Subtle distractions almost entirely disappear; attention is unwavering and exclusive together with vivid mindfulness.

prompts.subtleFade: Did subtle thoughts fade entirely at any point?
prompts.wholeBody: Could you sense the breath across the whole body?

s7.title: Stage 7 - Exclusive Attention and Unifying the Mind
s7.short: Exclusive attention and unifying the mind
s7.goal: Reach effortlessly sustained exclusive attention and powerful mindfulness.
s7.obstacles: Effort is still required; boredom, restlessness, and doubt can arise. Bizarre sensations and involuntary movements may appear. Knowing when to drop effort is its own challenge.
s7.skills: Patient, diligent practice. Periodically relaxing effort on purpose to test whether it's still needed. Letting go of the need to control.
s7.mastery: Effort can be dropped entirely and the mind still maintains unprecedented stability and clarity - mental pliancy.

prompts.droppedEffort: Did you try dropping effort during this sit?
prompts.sensations: Were there sensations or movements that surprised you?

s8.title: Stage 8 - Mental Pliancy and Pacifying the Senses
s8.short: Mental pliancy and pacifying the senses
s8.goal: Complete pacification of the senses and the full arising of meditative joy.
s8.obstacles: Unusual sensations, energy currents, and intense joy can themselves become distracting.
s8.skills: Effortless attention and continuous introspective awareness. Let extraordinary experiences be - they're part of unification, not problems to fix.
s8.mastery: Body suffused with comfort, mental state of intense joy; you can sit for hours without dullness, distraction, or discomfort.

prompts.unusual: Anything unusual to note from today?
prompts.comfort: Did the body settle into a state of comfort?

s9.title: Stage 9 - Calming the Intensity of Meditative Joy
s9.short: Calming the intensity of meditative joy
s9.goal: Mature meditative joy into tranquility and equanimity.
s9.obstacles: The intensity of joy can perturb the mind and disrupt practice.
s9.skills: Continued practice; allowing familiarity with joy to soften its intensity.
s9.mastery: Consistently evoking mental and physical pliancy with profound tranquility and equanimity.

prompts.joyShape: How did joy feel today - sharp, settled, or somewhere in between?
prompts.equanimity: Was there a sense of equanimity by the end of the sit?

s10.title: Stage 10 - Tranquility and Equanimity
s10.goal: Establish the qualities of śamatha as the normal condition of the mind.
s10.obstacles: The qualities of śamatha fade after the meditation session ends.
s10.skills: Continuing regular practice and bringing mindfulness into daily life so the qualities don't erode between sessions.
s10.mastery: The qualities of śamatha persist for many hours after rising from the cushion.

prompts.carried: Did anything from the sit carry into the rest of the day?
prompts.pulledOut: What pulled you out of it, if anything?
